title: Meeting Recovery Syndrome Is Ruining Your Workday
subtitle: 会议恢复综合症：正在吞噬你工作日的隐形杀手

paragraphs.en: Phyllis Hartman knows what it's like to make one's way through the depths of office meeting hell. Managers at one of her former human resources jobs arranged so many meetings that attendees would fall asleep at the table or intentionally arrive late. With hours of her day blocked up with unnecessary meetings, she was often forced to make up her work during overtime.
paragraphs.zh: 菲利斯·哈特曼深知深陷办公室会议地狱的滋味。她此前一份人力资源工作中，经理们安排了太多会议，以至于与会者会在桌边睡着，或者故意迟到。每天大量时间被不必要的会议占据，她常常被迫加班来弥补落下的工作。

paragraphs.en: She isn't alone in her frustration. Between 11 million and 55 million meetings are held each day in the United States, costing most organisations between 7% and 15% of their personnel budgets. Every week, employees spend about six hours in meetings, while the average manager meets for a staggering 23 hours.
paragraphs.zh: 她并非唯一感到沮丧的人。美国每天要举行1100万至5500万场会议，耗费了多数组织7%至15%的人力预算。员工每周开会时间约6小时，而经理的平均开会时间则高达惊人的23小时。

words.meaning: adj. 惊人的；令人震惊的

paragraphs.en: And though experts agree that traditional meetings are essential for making certain decisions, some employees view them as one of the most unnecessary parts of the workday. The result is not only hundreds of billions of wasted dollars, but an annoyance called "meeting recovery syndrome (MRS)": time spent cooling off and regaining focus after a useless meeting.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管专家认同传统会议对某些决策至关重要，但部分员工却将其视为工作日里最没必要的环节之一。其结果不仅是数千亿美元的浪费，还有一种被称为“会议恢复综合症”的烦恼：即在一场无用的会议之后，用来平复心情、重新集中注意力的时间。

paragraphs.en: Meeting recovery syndrome is a concept that should be familiar to almost anyone who has held a formal job. It isn't ground-breaking to say workers feel fatigued after a meeting, but only in recent decades have scientists deemed the condition worthy of further investigation. With its links to organisational efficiency and employee wellbeing, MRS has attracted the attention of psychologists.
paragraphs.zh: 会议恢复综合症这个概念，几乎任何上过班的人都应该感同身受。说员工会后感到疲劳并非什么创见，但直到最近几十年，科学家们才开始认为这一现象值得深入研究。由于与组织效率和员工福祉密切相关，会议恢复综合症已引起了心理学家的关注。

paragraphs.en: Today, MRS is most easily understood as a slow renewal of finite mental and physical resources. When an employee sits through an ineffective meeting their brain power is essentially being drained away. Meetings drain vitality if they last too long, fail to engage employees or turn into one-sided lectures.
paragraphs.zh: 如今，会议恢复综合症最易被理解为有限身心资源的缓慢恢复过程。当员工熬过一场低效会议时，其脑力基本上已被耗尽。如果会议持续时间过长、无法调动员工参与或变成单向灌输的讲座，它就会消耗人的活力。

paragraphs.en: The conservation of resources theory states that psychological stress occurs when a person's resources are threatened or lost. In the case of office meetings, where employees' most valuable resources are their focus and motivation, this can mean an abrupt halt in productivity as they take time to recover. As humans, when we transition from one task to another, it takes an effortful cognitive switch.
paragraphs.zh: 资源守恒理论指出，当一个人的资源受到威胁或丧失时，心理压力便会产生。在办公室会议中，员工最宝贵的资源就是注意力和动力，这意味着他们需要花时间恢复，生产力也会骤然停滞。作为人类，当我们从一项任务切换到另一项时，需要付出努力的认知转换。

paragraphs.en: If we are already drained to dangerous levels, then making the mental switch to the next thing is extra tough. It's common to see people cyber-loafing after a frustrating meeting, going and getting coffee, or interrupting a colleague to vent. Each person's ability to recover from horrible meetings is different.
paragraphs.zh: 如果我们已经被消耗到了危险水平，那么将思绪切换到下一件事就格外困难。经常能看到人们在一场令人沮丧的会议后上网摸鱼、去喝杯咖啡，或者打断同事抱怨一番。每个人从糟糕会议中恢复的能力各不相同。

paragraphs.en: Some can bounce back quickly, while others carry their fatigue until the end of the workday. Switching tasks in a non-MRS condition takes about 10 to 15 minutes. With MRS, it may take as long as 45 minutes on average.
paragraphs.zh: 有些人能迅速回血，有些人则会带着疲惫撑到下班。在非会议恢复综合症状态下切换任务大约需要10到15分钟。而在会议恢复综合症影响下，恢复时间平均可能长达45分钟。

paragraphs.en: It's even worse when a worker has several meetings that are separated by 30 minutes. Researcher Joseph Allen notes there isn't enough time to transition or recover before the next meeting, compounding the problem. In an effort to combat the side effects of MRS, Allen and colleagues published a study detailing the best ways to avoid common traps.
paragraphs.zh: 若员工有数场间隔仅30分钟的会议，情况就更糟了。研究员约瑟夫·艾伦指出，根本没有足够时间在下一场会议前完成过渡或恢复，这会使问题雪上加霜。为应对会议恢复综合症的副作用，艾伦及其同事发表了一项研究，详细阐述了避免常见陷阱的最佳方法。

paragraphs.en: Mroz says a good place to start is asking ourselves if our meetings are even necessary in the first place. If all that's on the agenda is a quick catch-up, it may better suit the group to send around an email instead. "The second thing I would always recommend is keep the meeting as small as possible," says Mroz.
paragraphs.zh: 姆罗兹表示，一个好的起点是首先问问自己，这场会议是否真的有必要。如果议程上只是简单的进度同步，那发一封邮件可能对团队更合适。“我始终推荐的第二点是，尽量让会议规模小一些，”姆罗兹说。

paragraphs.en: Less time in meetings would ultimately lead to more employee engagement in the meetings they do attend, which experts agree is a proven remedy for MRS. Employees also feel taxed when they are invited to meetings that don't inspire participation, says Cliff Scott. It takes precious time for them to vent and try to regain focus after a pointless meeting.
paragraphs.zh: 减少会议时间最终会提高员工对必须参加的会议的投入度，专家认为这是针对会议恢复综合症的行之有效的补救措施。组织学教授克利夫·斯科特表示，当员工被邀请参加那些无法激发参与感的会议时，他们也会感到身心俱疲。在一场毫无意义的会议后，他们需要耗费宝贵时间来发泄情绪并试图重新集中注意力。

paragraphs.en: Despite the relative scarcity of research, Hartman has taught herself many of the same tricks and has come a long way. She now invites not just essential employees but also representatives from every department that might have a stake in the issue. Managers who seek input even from non-experts can find greater support and cooperation.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管相关研究相对匮乏，哈特曼还是自学了许多类似的技巧，并取得了长足进步。如今，她邀请参会的人不仅包括核心员工，还有与议题可能相关的各部门代表。那些甚至向非专业人士征询意见的管理者，往往能从员工那里获得更多支持与合作。

paragraphs.en: If an organisation were to apply all 22 suggestions from the findings, the most noticeable difference would be a stark decrease in the total number of meetings. Allen says one trick with promise is for employees to identify things that quickly change their mood from negative to positive. Finding a personal happy place and going there might be key to facilitating recovery.
paragraphs.zh: 如果组织采纳研究结果中的全部22条建议，最显著的变化将是会议总数的大幅减少。艾伦表示，一个有前景的技巧是让员工找出能迅速将自己的情绪从消极转为积极的事物。找到一个属于自己的快乐之地并沉浸其中，或许是促进恢复的关键。

paragraphs.en: Leaders should see themselves as "stewards of everyone else's valuable time", adds Steven Rogelberg. Having the skills to foresee potential traps and treat employees' endurance with care allows leaders to provide effective deterrents to MRS. Most important, however, is for organisations to awaken to the concept of meetings being flexible.
paragraphs.zh: 《会议的惊人科学》一书作者史蒂文·罗格尔伯格补充道，领导者应视自己为“他人宝贵时间的管理者”。具备预见潜在陷阱并珍视员工耐力的能力，领导者就能对会议恢复综合症形成有效威慑。然而，最重要的是，组织必须认识到会议应当具有灵活性。

paragraphs.en: By reshaping the way they prioritise employees' time, companies can eliminate the very sources of MRS in their tracks.
paragraphs.zh: 通过重塑优先安排员工时间的方式，企业可以从根源上消除会议恢复综合症的产生土壤。
